regions.text: The thinking of scientists has become clearer and truer as their instruments have become better. Galileo made one of the first microscopes as well as the first telescope.
By the middle of the seventeenth century this new instrument had opened up to man another world, a world of things too small for our eyes by themselves to see.

The microscope made possible the discovery of the cell structure of all living things. Even the body of man himself could be pictured now as a great machine with millions of working parts, each part a cell.
structure: how a thing is made up; the way its parts are put together. The structure of the plant stem is made clearer by the microscope.
